pre_mortem_question: P1 crypto_wizards_capture: What is the most likely downside risk we are accepting by skipping this?
failure_mode: Selection decisions may be based on incomplete spread/score history, producing false positives and pairs that are untradeable in practice.
prevention: Require capture completeness on spread/z-score/ECM metrics before any research promotion.

required_proof: production-eligible strategy with required two-leg base/stress results across multiple pairs
pre_mortem_question: P2 strategy_acceptance: If we move to execution without this, what hard failure would most likely break us first?
failure_mode: A strategy might appear good in dashboards but be rejected by production-style gates after deployment.
prevention: Keep strategy acceptance gates as mandatory before moving any pair into execution lanes.

required_proof: submit flag, credentials, SDK, indexer, and authenticated order adapter all ready
pre_mortem_question: P3 dydx_testnet_readiness: What would fail and what signal would we watch first?
failure_mode: Paper/live requests can fail at submit time and silently degrade into partial or dropped hedges.
prevention: Keep all venue execution checks and adapter wiring as hard blockers before paper/live signals.

pre_mortem_question: P4 paper_execution_gate: What would fail and what signal would we watch first?
failure_mode: Paper gating might pass with assumptions that do not hold in real or execution-tied backtests.
prevention: Run paper preflight as a strict step with explicit block reasons and no override path.

required_proof: paper journal or trade store contains outcome events for later modeling
pre_mortem_question: P5 learning_event_store: What is the most likely downside risk we are accepting by skipping this?
failure_mode: No realized outcomes means no feedback loop; model quality and execution bias drift undetected.
prevention: Collect learning outcomes before model score updates and prevent model retraining on missing labels.
